family_audit: Round 26 confirmed legacy::port_grouped_row_traversal_into_the_non_ptx_128x128_sibling as a valid correct alternate surface, but not as a new headroom family: runtime recovered to 24.183295 ms while tensor activity, warps active, barrier, and scoreboard all returned to the same plateau band.
Kept legacy::retune_the_auxiliary_256x128_hot_band_k_loop_schedule closed after its round-25 loss because it failed to move the machine state away from the 24.16-24.19 ms plateau.

Kept legacy::retune_the_active_ptx_one_k_128x128_hot_band_control_path deprioritized because the family is still frozen after repeated flats and this round should restore the cleanest measured winner before trying more exploratory branches.

directions.name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
directions.hypothesis: Round 26 answered the main question about the grouped-row non-PTX sibling: it is a stable alternate surface, but it does not break the plateau. Runtime recovered from 24.690687 ms to 24.183295 ms, yet the machine state came back almost exactly to the same 48.34%-tensor / 16.61%-warps / 5.49%-barrier / 7.23%-long-scoreboard envelope. That means the best next move is to restore the exact PTX winner again so the loop re-centers on the strongest measured anchor before it spends more rounds on tertiary surfaces.
directions.expected_bottleneck: No new bottleneck is being attacked here; this is the exact recovery path back to the best measured correct surface.
directions.risk: Low. This is the exact best measured surface and the safest way to erase the residual gap between the current alternate surface and the recorded winner.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
ranking_notes: This now ranks first because both the auxiliary 256x128 probe and the non-PTX sibling have failed to open a new machine-state regime.
The frontier also agrees: after the round-26 measurement, the exact PTX restore is again the highest-priority live representative.
The right use of round 27 is to re-anchor on the cleanest measured winner before spending more budget on lower-confidence branches.
directions.stop_condition: Stop once the exact PTX winner surface is restored or if a remeasurement shows that it no longer reproduces the prior 24.16-24.18 ms band.

directions.name: Restore The Grouped-Row Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling Surface
directions.hypothesis: The grouped-row non-PTX sibling remains the best correctness-proven alternate surface after the PTX winner. Round 26 showed that it can recover quickly from a bad source and stay in the plateau band, which still makes it the right second-ranked branch if the loop wants a like-for-like alternate surface without dropping into a noisier historical family.
directions.expected_bottleneck: PTX-microkernel-specific control and export coupling on the current winner surface, while preserving the same broad 128x128 footprint and grouped-row locality.
directions.risk: Low to medium. This is an exact restore of a measured-correct alternate surface, but the expected upside is now clearly bounded by the plateau it just reproduced.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
ranking_notes: This stays second because it is still the strongest alternate surface after the PTX winner.
It drops below dir_01 because round 26 showed that the sibling surface is recoverable but not superior.
Keep it live in the queue so the search does not collapse entirely into only one PTX surface.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the sibling surface continues to reproduce the same plateau signature without any separation from the PTX winner.

directions.name: Restore Accepted Grouped-Rows-8 Hot-Band Consumer Ordering
directions.hypothesis: The third slot should still preserve one more alternate PTX surface that changes grouping and consumer locality more materially than another frozen micro-retime. The grouped_rows=8 surface already has a measured loss, so it is not a primary branch, but it remains a better queue-width placeholder than reopening the frozen active PTX control family yet again.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Grouped-row traversal and consumer-order locality in the PTX hot-band path, not the exact same surface restore targeted by dir_01.
directions.risk: Medium. This is still a historically slower PTX alternate with one measured loss, so it is being kept only for queue breadth.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
ranking_notes: This remains the third-ranked queue-width branch after the two strongest exact restores.
It outranks another frozen PTX control retime because it still changes the surface more materially.
Use it only if the search wants one more alternate PTX state after re-anchoring on the winner.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the grouped_rows=8 restore remains materially behind both dir_01 and dir_02 without a cleaner scheduler signature.

notes: Round 27 interprets the non-PTX sibling as a correct plateau alternate, not as a new leader. The ranking therefore re-centers the exact PTX winner while keeping the sibling and grouped_rows=8 surfaces alive as lower-ranked alternate states.
